steps.title: Partner Farm Selection
steps.shortDescription: We work directly with certified organic farms in specific growing regions of India.
steps.fullDescription: Every ingredient begins with a relationship. We partner directly with certified organic farms in India's traditional growing regions—Kerala for ginger, Rajasthan for ashwagandha, Tamil Nadu for turmeric. These aren't anonymous suppliers; they're partners we've visited, vetted, and work with year after year.

steps.title: Soil & Climate Assessment
steps.shortDescription: Understanding the growing environment that shapes each ingredient.
steps.fullDescription: The potency of a plant depends on its environment. We assess soil composition, climate patterns, and seasonal cycles to ensure optimal growing conditions. Ashwagandha from Rajasthan's arid soil naturally produces higher withanolide concentrations—this isn't by chance, it's by design.

steps.title: Cultivation Monitoring
steps.shortDescription: Observing growth practices throughout the cultivation period.
steps.fullDescription: Organic certification is the baseline, not the ceiling. We monitor cultivation practices throughout the growing season—irrigation methods, pest management (only organic-approved methods), and timing of interventions. Quality at harvest depends on quality throughout growth.

steps.title: Harvest Timing
steps.shortDescription: Harvesting at peak potency for maximum active compound content.
steps.fullDescription: Timing matters. Ginger is harvested when gingerol content peaks. Ashwagandha roots are collected after 150-180 days. Turmeric rhizomes at 7-9 months. Each plant has an optimal harvest window, and we work with farmers to ensure precise timing.

steps.title: Post-Harvest Handling
steps.shortDescription: Immediate processing to preserve integrity and prevent degradation.
steps.fullDescription: The window between harvest and processing is critical. Raw materials are cleaned, sorted, and prepared for drying within hours of harvest. Improper handling at this stage can compromise months of careful cultivation.

steps.title: Transportation & Chain of Custody
steps.shortDescription: Documented transfer from farm to facility with full traceability.
steps.fullDescription: From farm to our facility, every transfer is documented. We maintain chain of custody records that trace each batch back to its origin farm, harvest date, and handling conditions. This isn't bureaucracy—it's accountability.

steps.title: Identity Testing
steps.shortDescription: Confirming each ingredient is exactly what it claims to be.
steps.fullDescription: Before anything enters production, we verify identity. Using HPTLC (High-Performance Thin-Layer Chromatography) and other analytical methods, we confirm that the material is exactly what it claims to be. Adulteration is common in the supplement industry—we test to ensure it never enters our supply chain.

steps.title: Potency Analysis
steps.shortDescription: Measuring active compound concentrations to ensure effectiveness.
steps.fullDescription: Identity isn't enough—we need to know potency. Using HPLC (High-Performance Liquid Chromatography), we quantify active compounds: gingerols in ginger, withanolides in ashwagandha, curcuminoids in turmeric. Materials that don't meet our potency specifications are rejected.

steps.title: Contaminant Screening
steps.shortDescription: Testing for heavy metals, pesticides, and environmental toxins.
steps.fullDescription: Clean ingredients require clean testing. Every incoming material is screened for heavy metals (lead, mercury, arsenic, cadmium), pesticide residues, and other environmental contaminants. Our limits are often stricter than regulatory requirements.

steps.title: Microbial Testing
steps.shortDescription: Ensuring freedom from harmful microorganisms.
steps.fullDescription: Botanical materials can harbor microorganisms. We test for total aerobic count, yeast and mold, and specific pathogens including E. coli and Salmonella. Clean raw materials lead to clean finished products.

steps.title: Certificate of Analysis Review
steps.shortDescription: Documentation review and approval before production release.
steps.fullDescription: Before any material enters production, our quality team reviews all test results and documentation. This isn't a formality—it's a checkpoint. Materials are either approved for use or rejected. There is no 'conditional' acceptance.

steps.title: Extraction & Standardization
steps.shortDescription: Creating concentrated extracts with consistent active compound levels.
steps.fullDescription: Raw herbs become standardized extracts through careful extraction. We use appropriate solvents (water, ethanol, or other food-grade options depending on the compound) to concentrate active ingredients to consistent, specified levels.
details: Solvent selection based on target compounds

steps.title: Particle Size Optimization
steps.shortDescription: Milling to precise particle sizes for optimal absorption.
steps.fullDescription: Particle size affects everything from blend uniformity to absorption. Extracts are milled to specified particle size ranges, ensuring consistent behavior during blending and optimal performance in the finished product.

steps.title: Blending & Homogenization
steps.shortDescription: Combining ingredients for uniform distribution in every capsule.
steps.fullDescription: Multiple ingredients must be perfectly blended. Using V-blenders and other equipment, we achieve homogeneous mixtures where every portion contains the same ratio of ingredients. We verify blend uniformity through sampling and testing.

steps.title: In-Process Quality Checks
steps.shortDescription: Continuous monitoring throughout the processing stage.
steps.fullDescription: Quality isn't just tested at the end—it's monitored throughout. In-process checks verify weight, appearance, and other parameters at multiple points during processing. Problems are caught and corrected in real-time.

steps.title: Capsule Formulation
steps.shortDescription: Filling plant-based capsules with precise dosages.
steps.fullDescription: The blended extract is filled into plant-based HPMC capsules (no animal gelatin). Automatic filling machines ensure precise, consistent fill weights. Each capsule contains exactly what the label states.

steps.title: Weight Verification
steps.shortDescription: Statistical sampling to verify fill weight accuracy.
steps.fullDescription: After filling, we verify weights through statistical sampling. Capsules are weighed individually and compared to specifications. Batches that don't meet weight uniformity standards don't move forward.

steps.title: Disintegration Testing
steps.shortDescription: Ensuring capsules break down properly for absorption.
steps.fullDescription: A capsule is only effective if it disintegrates properly in the body. We test disintegration time using USP apparatus, ensuring capsules break down within specified timeframes under simulated digestive conditions.

steps.title: Stability Testing
steps.shortDescription: Confirming potency is maintained throughout shelf life.
steps.fullDescription: We don't guess at shelf life—we test it. Samples are stored under accelerated and real-time conditions, then tested at intervals to verify that potency, appearance, and other quality attributes remain within specification throughout the labeled shelf life.

steps.title: Final Product Testing
steps.shortDescription: Comprehensive testing of the finished product.
steps.fullDescription: Before release, finished products undergo comprehensive testing: identity, potency, contaminants, and microbial limits. This is the final verification that everything upstream worked correctly.

steps.title: Third-Party Verification
steps.shortDescription: Independent laboratory testing for additional assurance.
steps.fullDescription: We don't just trust our own testing—we verify with independent third-party laboratories. External testing provides additional assurance that our internal results are accurate and that the product meets all specifications.

steps.title: Batch Release & Documentation
steps.shortDescription: Final approval and complete traceability records.
steps.fullDescription: Only after all testing is complete and reviewed does a batch receive release approval. Every batch is fully documented—from farm origin to finished product testing. This documentation is retained and available for review. Quality is not just claimed; it is recorded.

standards.name: cGMP (21 CFR Part 111)
standards.description: FDA regulations for dietary supplement manufacturing, ensuring identity, purity, strength, and composition.

standards.name: FSSC 22000
standards.description: Global food safety management certification built on ISO 22000, with additional requirements for manufacturing.

standards.name: ISO 22000:2018
standards.description: International standard for food safety management, covering all aspects of the supply chain.

standards.name: USP Standards
standards.description: Recognized standards for identity, strength, quality, and purity of medicines and supplements.
